concerns.text: T076 conflates Constitution Principle III (input data checksums) and IV (output artifact checksums) into a single task. This violates the principle of single responsibility and the Constitution's explicit separation of concerns. Separate tasks are needed for each principle.

T083's verification of the Quickstart guide only checks execution *time*, not whether a *novice user* can successfully complete the guide. This fails to verify the intended usability goal and is a weak test of FR-028.

T044 only implements subsampling for domain bias adjustment (FR-027), omitting the alternative of flagging a violation. The task description states 'either subsample... or flag', but the implementation only covers one option.
T026 incorrectly lists T028 as a dependency. Synthetic data generation (T026) should not depend on power analysis (T028); these are independent steps.
T042 conflates Bonferroni correction (for subgroup analysis) with sensitivity analysis. These are distinct requirements and should be addressed in separate steps.
SC-001 and SC-031b both require extraction accuracy validation on the same dataset, but with different thresholds (95% vs 85%). This inconsistency should be resolved.
The plan states a memory limit of '≤7GB RAM' but the spec (FR-009) mandates '≤2 GB'. This discrepancy needs resolution.
The plan's description of T076 and T077 is confusing and redundant. Clarify the distinct responsibilities of each task regarding checksumming input vs. output artifacts.
The description of FR-031b is overly verbose and could be more concise. Focus on the core requirement of evaluating extraction accuracy with human-verified ground truth.
